Title: An Adaptive Regime-Based Framework for Modeling and Forecasting Non-Stationary Retail Sales Data
Description: This project addresses the fundamental flaw in traditional retail analytics: the assumption of "local stability" in fixed rolling windows. By recognizing that retail environments are dynamic and prone to structural changes—such as promotions, holidays, and global shocks—this research moves beyond simple data smoothing. The framework identifies distinct "regimes" where sales behavior is statistically stable, allowing for models that are not only more accurate in their predictions but also deeply interpretable regarding how specific factors (covariates) influence sales during different market states.
Keywords: Retail Analytics , Regime Detection , Structural Breaks , Adaptive Rolling Windows , Model Interpretability
My Contribution: I am the lead researcher and data science expert in the research. I contributed to this reserch by applying both my statistical knowledge and technical know-how in algorithm development and ensuring the conformity of the algorithm to statistical assumptions and guidelines.
Methodologies: Partitioning time series into contiguous and data-driven windows , defining regimes as statistically stable time intervals , identifying structural changes through shifts in mean and variability , utilizing a regime detection engine based on the z-score technique , applying a sensitivity threshold to declare regime breaks , ensuring a minimum window size for statistical reliability , employing chronological splitting within each regime to prevent data leakage , fitting local regression models to capture regime-specific covariate effects, calculating Within-Regime Variance Ratio (WRV) to assess segmentation efficiency, and using Inter-Regime Mean Separation (IMS) to validate structural breaks .

Title: A Study on the Effects of Some Macroeconomic Variables on Economic Growth Indicators in Nigeria
Description: This project studies the relationship between key economic variables- FPI, Exchange Rate, and Food Import Share, and some economic growth indicators such as GDP, Food Inflation Rate, and Total Inflation Rate.
Keywords: Macroeconomic Variables, Multivariate Analysis, Correlation Analysis, Line Plot
My Contribution: As the lead researcher, I utilized multivariate analysis to model the relationships and further utilized canonical correlation to measure the degree of relationship between two sets of variables. My contribution extends to technical writing of the reports, and using R-programing for statistical computing.
Methodologies: Multivariate Analysis to study the impact of multiple independent variables on more than 1 dependent variables, Canonical Correlation to understand the relationship between the two groups, Normality of Errors Test to check for the core assumptions in multivariate analysis, Multivariate Analysis of variance to test for difference of mean and significance impacts on the dependent variables using Wilks statistic.  And evaluation metrics such as coefficient of dtermination, F-statistic, Adjusted coefficient of determination were utilized to check the efficiency of the developed models.
